vulnerability.title: Disable GSSAPI Authentication
vulnerability.description: Unless needed, SSH should not permit extraneous or unnecessary authentication mechanisms like GSSAPI. The default SSH configuration disallows authentications based on GSSAPI. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for GSSAPIAuthentication. To explicitly disable GSSAPI authentication, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: GSSAPIAuthentication no
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*GSSAPIAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*GSSAPIAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*GSSAPIAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/

triage_decision.reason: Disabling GSSAPI authentication is a standard SSH hardening practice that reduces the attack surface by removing unnecessary authentication mechanisms. It does not impact standard password or key-based authentication.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential loss of Kerberos-based single sign-on (SSO) if the environment relies on GSSAPI for SSH authentication

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan is sound and directly addresses the vulnerability by utilizing the recommended include directory for configuration overrides. By creating/updating a specific file in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/`, the remediation follows modern Linux configuration management practices and ensures the setting persists even if the main `sshd_config` is updated by package managers.
concerns: The plan assumes `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` is configured to include files from `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf`. While this is standard on most modern distributions (Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL/CentOS), if the `Include` directive is missing from the main config, the changes in the `.d` directory will be ignored.
suggested_improvements: Add a step to verify that the `Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf` directive exists in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` before proceeding.
Perform a configuration syntax check using `sshd -t` before restarting the service to prevent service downtime in case of a typo.
